query: What was the P95 search latency before and after the fix in January?
groundTruth: Required: before the fix the P95 had regressed to roughly 850ms (January 22 benchmark measured 870ms); after the fix the January 24 benchmark showed P95 = 115ms. Good-to-have: the pre-regression baseline was 120ms.

query: How much did the Jenkins to GitHub Actions migration save per year?
groundTruth: Approximately $9,600 per year in EC2 costs (the decommissioned Jenkins EC2 instance had cost about $800/month).

query: What were the load test results at 500 concurrent users?
groundTruth: At 500 concurrent users (the ramp-up scenario): P50 = 88ms, P95 = 410ms, P99 = 780ms; throughput 2,890 requests/second; error rate 0.08%; app-server CPU peaked at 78% and RDS CPU peaked at 52%. The /api/search endpoint degraded first, with P95 reaching 1,100ms at peak load.

goldFacts.label: /api/search degraded first, P95 1,100ms at peak load

expectedTasks: Auth Token Expiry Bug — Investigation & Fix
query: What was the root cause of the auth token expiry bug?
groundTruth: During the v2.1 deployment on December 14, 2025, an environment-variable rename broke the token TTL config: JWT_ACCESS_TTL_SECONDS was renamed to JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_TTL in the codebase, but the production environment was not updated. The token-generation code then fell back to a hardcoded default of 300 seconds (5 minutes) with no warning log, so access tokens expired after 5 minutes instead of the configured 4 hours (14400 seconds).

goldFacts.taskTitle: Auth Token Expiry Bug — Investigation & Fix
goldFacts.label: env var renamed JWT_ACCESS_TTL_SECONDS → JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_TTL

goldFacts.taskTitle: Auth Token Expiry Bug — Investigation & Fix
goldFacts.label: production environment not updated

goldFacts.taskTitle: Auth Token Expiry Bug — Investigation & Fix
goldFacts.label: fallback hardcoded 300 seconds default

query: Walk me through the full timeline of the January 15 outage from alert to full recovery.
groundTruth: Timeline (all times PST, January 15, 2026): 10:05 AM — the triggering deploy was released. 10:12 AM — the first PagerDuty alert fired (Sentry fatal error spike); start of the outage. 10:15 AM — Alice Wang acknowledged the alert and began investigating. 10:20 AM — Alice confirmed the API pods were crashlooping in Kubernetes (logs showed 'Error: Cannot find module ../config/db'). 10:25 AM — Alice identified that the 10:05 AM deploy included a file rename not reflected in an import path. 10:30 AM — Alice reverted the deploy via the rollback workflow. 10:38 AM — rollback completed, but the API did not recover because Kubernetes readiness probes were still failing for old pods. 10:40 AM — Bob joined and identified the stuck pods. 11:15 AM — manual pod restart was performed. 11:22 AM — the API recovered but with degraded performance (P95 = 1,200ms) due to cache warming. 12:52 PM — full recovery; P95 returned to baseline (< 200ms) as caches warmed. Total user-visible downtime was 2 hours 40 minutes (10:12 AM – 12:52 PM); cache degradation lasted an additional 90 minutes. The metrics-collector fix (PR #181) was merged and deployed at 2:30 PM with no incident.

goldFacts.label: 11:15 AM manual pod restart
goldFacts.startText: Manual pod restart was performed at 11:15 AM

goldFacts.label: 12:52 PM full recovery (P95 back to baseline)
2:30 PM metrics-collector fix deployed (PR #181)
goldFacts.startText: PR #181) was merged and deployed at 2:30 PM

query: What action items came out of the January 15 outage post-mortem?
groundTruth: All action items were assigned and completed by January 23, 2026: (1) Alice — add 'node --check' to the CI pipeline for all entry-point files (done January 16, PR #182); (2) Bob — add the metrics collector to the integration-test startup (done January 18, PR #184); (3) Alice — update the rollback runbook with explicit pod-restart steps (done January 20); (4) Charlie — add a Grafana alert for crashlooping pods (done January 17); (5) all — retrospective meeting held January 20 at 2:00 PM PST, with notes published to Confluence.

goldFacts.label: AI 2: Bob — metrics collector in integration tests (PR #184)
AI 4: Charlie — Grafana alert for crashlooping pods
AI 5: retrospective meeting held Jan 20 2:00 PM PST

Docker issue: the local Docker Compose setup for the test database required Docker Desktop 4.28+, but his laptop shipped with 4.26; Bob then updated CONTRIBUTING.md to add the minimum Docker Desktop version requirement. (Dev-environment setup was completed by 1:15 PM.)

query: What were the load test results and what bottlenecks were found?
groundTruth: Load test results (k6 v0.50, February 12, 2026, against staging): Steady load (200 users) — P50 = 45ms, P95 = 180ms, P99 = 320ms; throughput 1,240 req/s; error rate 0.02%. Ramp-up (500 users) — P50 = 88ms, P95 = 410ms, P99 = 780ms; throughput 2,890 req/s; error rate 0.08%; /api/search degraded first (P95 1,100ms). Spike (800 users) — P99 reached 4,200ms and error rate hit 2.3% (HTTP 503) in first 30s; after 45s auto-scaling launched a third server and P99 returned to 820ms. Bottlenecks: (1) search endpoint degrades first (partially addressed by Redis query cache, Feb 3); (2) auto-scaling took 45s to respond (minimum instance count raised from 2 to 3); (3) RDS connection pool exhausted during spike (max_connections=100, peak 97; pool size increased to 150). CPU metrics are supporting detail, not required.

goldFacts.label: bottleneck 1: search endpoint degrades first
goldFacts.startText: search endpoint is the first to degrade

goldFacts.label: bottleneck 3: RDS pool exhausted, raised 100→150
goldFacts.endText: pool size will be increased to 150

query: Tell me about the project.
groundTruth: The project is 'Project Atlas', described as an 'Internal developer platform to streamline engineering workflows, CI/CD, monitoring, and team collaboration.' It was created by Alice Wang. Members: Alice Wang (manager), Bob Chen (editor), and Charlie Li (editor). It contains 21 tasks spanning planning, infrastructure, features, security, and operations.

query: How long was the January 15 outage?
groundTruth: 2 hours and 40 minutes (10:12 AM – 12:52 PM PST on January 15, 2026).

query: What performance improvements were made this quarter?
groundTruth: Performance improvements made this quarter: (1) Search index optimization — the invalid GIN index on content_tsv was dropped and recreated on January 24, bringing search P95 from ~850ms back to 115ms; a Redis query-result cache (60-second TTL) added on February 3 further cut P50 to 12ms for cached queries and reduced average database load by 31%. (2) CI/CD migration (Jenkins to GitHub Actions) — pipeline startup time dropped from about 4 minutes to 45 seconds, average CI duration from 11 minutes to 4 minutes 10 seconds, and failure rate from 7.8% to 3.2%. (3) Load-test-driven capacity changes — minimum app-server count was raised from 2 to 3 on February 25, and the RDS connection pool was increased from 100 to 150 on February 22. Enabling ElastiCache for session storage was planned for March 5, not yet completed in the seed.

goldFacts.label: search P95 850→115ms after GIN index recreate
Redis query cache cut P50 to 12ms (Feb 3)
pipeline startup 4 min → 45 seconds
goldFacts.startText: Pipeline startup time reduced to 45 seconds

goldFacts.label: CI duration 11 min → 4 m 10 s, failure 7.8% → 3.2%
goldFacts.startText: average CI pipeline duration 4 minutes 10 seconds

goldFacts.label: min app server count raised 2 → 3 (Feb 25)
goldFacts.startText: raise minimum app server count to 3

query: What did Bob Chen work on in February?
groundTruth: In February 2026 Bob Chen worked on: (1) API Rate Limiting (lead) — led the February 3 design review and opened draft PR #214 on February 5. (2) Jenkins → GitHub Actions migration (lead) — completed Phase 2 (staging deploy pipeline) on February 5, completed Phase 3 (production deploy pipeline) on February 24, and decommissioned the Jenkins instance on February 28. (3) Stripe payment integration (lead) — started integration work on February 3 and ran testing February 24–28. (4) Analytics Dashboard v2 (lead) — completed the API backend (three /api/v1/analytics/ endpoints) on February 28. (5) Marcus Chen's onboarding — ran the dev-environment setup session on February 1 and updated CONTRIBUTING.md with the minimum Docker Desktop version. (6) Atlas Core service registry (infrastructure) — attended the February 10 design kickoff, prepared the cost analysis on February 12 (approved by Alice on February 13), and was assigned to provision the MongoDB collection and Redis key namespace on staging in Phase 1 (starting February 17). He was also a contributor to the OpenAPI rewrite (rate-limiting and auth sections), though the seed gives no specific February date for that work.

query: What technologies were evaluated and rejected for the service registry, and why?
groundTruth: For storage, etcd was rejected (operational complexity) and Consul was rejected (licensing cost at scale) — MongoDB was chosen. For the communication protocol, gRPC was evaluated and rejected (on February 10, 2026) because most Atlas services are Node.js-based and the Node.js gRPC ecosystem requires native bindings that complicate Docker builds — REST over HTTPS was chosen.

goldFacts.taskTitle: Atlas Core Service Registry — Technical Design
goldFacts.label: etcd rejected: operational complexity
